message:
fix(indexer): tolerate metadata-only contention at rebuild tail (zz8ni)

cass index --full --force-rebuild was reporting {"success":false} after
a fully-committed multi-minute rebuild because the tail-end
last_indexed_at / last_scan_ts write in persist_final_index_run_metadata
lost the ephemeral-writer retry race against a peer cass process, and
bubbled that error out as a run failure. Operators and agents saw
"unhealthy / stale" indefinitely and re-triggered another doomed run,
discarding the already-durable index work.

Change:
- Split persist_final_index_run_metadata into a thin wrapper that
  constructs the ephemeral-writer closure and an injectable inner
  helper persist_final_index_run_metadata_with_writer that owns the
  log-on-success / log-and-swallow-on-failure policy.
- On success: emit the existing INFO markers for scan_start_ts and
  now_ms unchanged so status tooling keeps seeing them.
- On retry-exhausted failure: emit a structured tracing::warn! with
  db_path, performed_scan, scan_start_ts, now_ms, and the underlying
  error message, then return Ok. The index and lexical artifacts are
  already durable; a subsequent cass index --json incremental run
  will rewrite the markers once contention clears.

This matches the documented workaround in the cass skill (the data is
good; don't re-run; a trivial incremental writes the marker) and
eliminates OPEN issue #196's likely root cause where a prior failed
tail write left an un-written freshness marker that made every
subsequent incremental serialize against the same contention.

Tests:
- persist_final_index_run_metadata_logs_warning_and_returns_ok_when_retries_exhausted:
  injects an Err returning "database is busy" through the new
  injectable writer; asserts Ok result, the deferred-update warn log,
  the surfaced error message, and crucially that the success INFO log
  does NOT fire (no spurious claim of success).
- persist_final_index_run_metadata_with_writer_logs_success_info_when_writer_succeeds:
  injects Ok(()); asserts both scan_ts and indexed_at INFO logs fire
  and the deferred-update warn does NOT.
- Existing two persist_final_index_run_metadata_ tests continue to
  pass unchanged.

No rusqlite, no file deletion, no behavior change on the happy path.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
